title: Market Brief — Wednesday, May 20, 2026
summary: Compute is in severe shortage across every GPU class. Util-avail stands at just 9% for H100 and B200, 10% for H200, and 13% for A100, all tightening 4.5 to 7.2 percentage points over 30 days. H100 spot jumped 10% day-over-day to $2.64/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack, signaling acute demand pressure on the workhorse training GPU.
paragraphs: Compute is in severe shortage across every GPU class. Util-avail stands at just 9% for H100 and B200, 10% for H200, and 13% for A100, all tightening 4.5 to 7.2 percentage points over 30 days. H100 spot jumped 10% day-over-day to $2.64/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack, signaling acute demand pressure on the workhorse training GPU.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $9.65/M tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are selling at massive losses, burning approximately $9.20 and $9.33 per million tokens respectively. Grades: OpenAI Neutral (wide spread from premium o3 Pro to loss-leader gpt-oss), Anthropic Strong (Opus 4.6 holds $25/M premium), Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 is overvalued at $5.00/hr spot with 49% of NeoCloud offerings sitting idle, despite 91% Ornn utilization, suggesting listed inventory is mispriced against actual clearing rates. A100 is undervalued for inference workloads at $1.07/hr with the lowest TCO-adjusted serving cost of $4.57/M and the best margin profile, making it the buy for Llama-class deployment.
bullets: 9% util-avail on H100 marks the tightest compute supply across all tracked GPU classes.
13% util-avail on A100 still qualifies as severe shortage despite being the loosest tier available.
$2.64/hr H100 spot is up 10% day-over-day, the largest single-day GPU price move in today's data.
21.76 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.06/M output for Meta Llama 3.1 8B is the cheapest token price across all 53 tracked models.
160.6% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest GPU contract to hold short-term.
